gauge_source: Environment and Climate Change Canada 08MA002 Chilko River at Outlet of Chilko Lake

derivation.method: core summer whitewater season (June-September) monthly climatology of the lake-outlet gauge mapped to three runnable bands
derivation.routing_caveat: 08MA002 is at the Chilko Lake outlet, upstream of the Chilko River Lodge put-in; it does not include the tributary gain between the lake and Lava Canyon, so the true reach discharge at the rapid is somewhat higher than these lake-outlet values. Downstream 08MA001 (near Redstone) is below the Taseko confluence and over-states the pre-confluence Chilko flow, so it cannot define the band either. These bands are an honest lower-bound-leaning interpretation from the single upstream gauge, not gauge-routed reach discharge.

flow_bands.notes: 08MA002 September median (~67 m3/s): the lower runnable end of the summer window; the continuous wave train reads bonier with more exposed boulder controls and thinner recovery.
08MA002 August median (~105 m3/s): the mid-summer reference band at which the Lava Canyon / White Mile continuous Class IV wave train is classically expressed.
08MA002 July p90 (~142.6 m3/s): high summer freshet near the top of the runnable window; the White Mile large waves and laterals build and recovery windows shrink.
